Print This Page
Cambridge City
MA

Resolution
RES 2025 #292
Order Adopted
Dec 22, 2025 5:30 PM

Congratulations to Patrol Officer Nicole Pacheco on her retirement from The Cambridge Police Department

Information

Department:City Clerk's Office, PTSponsors:Councillor Paul F. Toner, Councillor Marc C. McGovern, Councillor Ayesha M. Wilson, Councillor E. Denise Simmons
Category:Congratulations

Attachments

Version History
  1. Printout

Body

WHEREAS:

The City Council has learned that Patrol Officer Nicole Pacheco will retire on February 3, 2026, after 31 years of dedication to the City of Cambridge and its residents; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Officer Pacheco was hired as a Police Officer on April 26, 1995, and proudly served the community she was born and raised in, coming full circle as a graduate of Cambridge Rindge and Latin School (CRLS) who later went on to serve Cambridge youth and families with distinction; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Officer Pacheco served in several units, including Operations and Traffic, but is most notably recognized for her service within the Family and Social Justice Unit, a unit that works above and beyond traditional juvenile policing by providing early intervention, direct engagement with youth and families, collaboration with schools and community partners, and support services addressing mental health, substance use, and other challenges affecting young people; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

As a Youth Resource Officer assigned to CRLS, Officer Pacheco worked closely with students, teen centers, afterschool programs, and community agencies, building trust, preventing crises, and supporting young people in their school and out-of-school environments, especially the middle-school and high-school students who depend on such connections; and

 

 

WHEREAS:

Officer Pacheco’s distinguished service is reflected in numerous recognitions that honor exceptional commitment to community policing, teamwork and service to the public; including the 2019 Letter of Commendation from Commissioner Bard, acknowledging her professionalism and exemplary performance, the 2011 Larry Burke Community Policing Award, which recognizes officers who building meaningful partnerships and trust within the community, the 2007 Commissioner Robert C. Haas Unit Ribbon Award given to units whose collaborative efforts significantly contributed to the safety and well-being of residents, and most recently the 2025 CAMMY Award at the Cambridge Jazz Festival, celebrating local individuals who make a meaningful difference in the community: now therefore be it

 

 

RESOLVED:

That the City Council go on record expressing its profound gratitude to Patrol Officer Nicole Pacheco for her decades of public service, her deep commitment to Cambridge youth and families, and her lasting contributions to community policing, and extending its warmest wishes for good health and happiness in her retirement; and be it further

 

 

RESOLVED:

That the City Clerk be and hereby is requested to forward a suitably engrossed copy of this resolution to Patrol Officer Nicole Pacheco on behalf of the entire City Council.

 

 

Meeting History

Dec 22, 2025 5:30 PM  City Council Regular Meeting
 Draft
RESULT:ORDER ADOPTED [UNANIMOUS]
YEAS:Burhan Azeem, Marc C. McGovern, Patricia Nolan, Sumbul Siddiqui, Jivan Sobrinho-Wheeler, Paul F. Toner, Ayesha M. Wilson, Catherine Zusy, E. Denise Simmons
